No Poverty.reason: The course provides foundational knowledge in financial services, which are crucial for ensuring access to economic resources for the poor (Target 1.4).

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and ending hunger or improving food systems.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a university-level finance course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and clean water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Modern investment strategies often include financing for clean energy technology and infrastructure, aligning with Target 7.a. This is a reasonable inference for an Investments course.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course on Investments is fundamental to understanding and facilitating economic growth (Target 8.1) and strengthening financial institutions to expand access to financial services (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Investments are intrinsically linked to providing financial services (Target 9.3) that fund the development of quality infrastructure (Target 9.1), industrialization, and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: An Investments course may tangentially cover the regulation and monitoring of financial markets and institutions, which relates to reducing inequalities (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Modern Investments courses often incorporate sustainable practices and ESG factors, which encourage companies to adopt sustainable practices (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: The field of Investments serves as a mechanism for mobilizing financial resources for climate action, aligning with Target 13.a.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or inferable connection between an Investments course and peace, justice, or strong institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The study of Investments directly relates to mobilizing financial resources (Target 17.3) and enhancing global macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13), which are key aspects of partnerships for the goals.
